markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify concurrent operator/model/validator actions create conflict/repair state instead of data loss. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: ParallelWriteConflictFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ParallelWriteConflictFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: /knowledge/crdt/updates/push now routes through durable save semantics; same-process same-document CRDT pushes serialize before head read; MT-237 fixture drives concurrent operator/model/validator HTTP pushes against managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger, proves one accepted head, two durable stale-base denials, conflict UI, HTTP repair writes, absent denied bytes, and EventLedger receipt linkage.
lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: HTTP parallel write conflict fixture passes against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and adversarial re-review approved RFV handoff. Existing bridge API round-trip check was attempted but stopped at repo-owned ENVIRONMENT_BLOCKED because POSTGRES_TEST_URL is not set.

adversarial_review.summary: No blocking correctness findings for MT-237. Prior bypassed-route and weak-fixture issues are closed: HTTP push goes through save_rich_document_draft, same-process same-document pushes serialize before head read, and the fixture asserts durable receipts, conflict UI, repair writes, denied-byte absence, and EventLedger linkage.
residual_risks: The mutex is in-process only. Cross-process backend writers could still race between EventLedger append and CRDT row insert; if multi-process backend writers enter scope, add a DB-level advisory lock or atomic transaction around event+row creation.
evidence: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --target-dir D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --test knowledge_parallel_write_conflict_fixture_tests -- --nocapture: PASS, 1 passed

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --target-dir D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --test knowledge_crdt_bridge_api_tests http_push_pull_round_trip_with_navigation_receipts -- --nocapture: ENVIRONMENT_BLOCKED, POSTGRES_TEST_URL not set
The MT-237 fixture uses the real axum CRDT route over loopback and the managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger fixture; no SQLite, Docker-default, external daemon, or mock-only proof is used.

Denied initial writes are asserted absent from both pull replay bytes and persisted PostgreSQL CRDT update bytes; conflict receipts are asserted against EventLedger event type, aggregate, actor, correlation, and payload.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
